AN NWS STORM SURVEY ASSESSED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 90 MPH TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY THREE MILES TO THE NORTHEAST ENDING ONE MILE WEST OF ST. MICHAEL. A GARAGE WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED AT A HOUSEHOLD NEAR THE END OF THE TRACK WHICH WAS DETERMINED TO BE THE MAXIMUM INTENSITY. MUCH OF THE OTHER DAMAGE WAS EF-0 IN INTENSITY. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-0 TORNADO TRACKED FROM NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF U.S. HIGHWAY 10 AND CENTRAL AVENUE IN BLAINE...TO THE NORTHWEST FOR APPROXIMATELY FIVE AND A HALF MILES...ENDING ONE HALF MILE NORTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY HIGHWAY 14 AND HANSON BOULEVARD IN FAR NORTHERN COON RAPIDS. THE DAMAGE WAS ALMOST ENTIRELY UPROOTED TREES...ON THREE OCCASIONS HAVING FALLEN ON HOUSES. THE START TIME IS AN APPROXIMATION AND MAY VARY BY A FEW MINUTES.
TORNADO REPORTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HANSON AND MAIN ST. TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN COON RAPIDS.

DELAYED REPORT. HEAVY RAIN ACROSS DOWNTOWN ST. CLOUD LED TO WATER HALF WAY UP VECHILES CAUSING 20 TO 30 TO BE STRANDED IN THE WATER. PEOPLE HELPED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT OUT OF THE VEHICLES.

TORNADO RATED EF-1. TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF THE BORDER WITH LYON COUNTY AND MOVED NORTHEAST TO 8 N MILROY. PATH LENGTH 2.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND A GRAIN BIN WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED.

TORNADO RATED EF-1. IT TOUCHED DOWN IN REDWOOD COUNTY... MOVED ACROSS A CORNER OF YELLOW MEDICINE COUNTY... BACK INTO REDWOOD COUNTY WHERE IT MOVED THROUGH BELVIEW... THEN INTO RENVILLE COUNTY... DISSIPATING NEAR 740TH AVENUE AND 240TH STREET AT 451 PM. PATH LENGTH 21 MILES AND MAXIMUM WIDTH 1/2 MILE. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...SOME ON HOMES. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS HEAVILY DAMAGED.

TORNADO RATED EF-0. IT TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF I-94 AND HIGHWAY 23. DOZENS OF TREES WERE BROKEN... TOPPLED OR PUSHED OVER AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST. IT DISSIPATED IN WAITE PARK...ABOUT 1.5 MILES WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 15 AND 23. TOTAL PATH LENGTH 2.3 MILES. IT DID NOT APPEAR THAT THE TORNADO WAS CONTINUOUSLY ON THE GROUND BUT THERE WERE FIVE DISTINCT LOCATIONS OF CONVERGENT DAMAGE ALONG ITS PATH.

NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMS EF-0 TORNADO. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN MILLE LACS COUNTY...ABOUT 6 MILES NORTHWEST OF DALBO...JUST NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD 10. IT THEN PROGRESSED SOUTHEAST INTO ISANTI COUNTY...CROSSING COUNTY ROAD 16 BEFORE TURNING SOUTH ALONG COUNTY ROAD 58. THE TORNADO ENDED NEAR COUNTY ROAD 58 LESS THAN HALF A MILE NORTH OF SOUTH STANCHFIELD LAKE. THE PATH LENGTH OF THIS TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES...AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 100 YARDS.
